commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: Christ did in fact desend, with respect to his soul, to those in hell so that he might come to aid those for whom it was necessary.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Foreseeing he (David) spoke of the resurrection of Christ. St. Peter shows them that the prophetical words of the Psalm, agree not to David in person, he being dead, and his body having remained in the grave, without rising from the dead. (Witham)

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: Again, His soul shall not be left in Hades, neither shall His flesh see corruption? "

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: This again is wonderful: it shows that His resurrection was not like that of other men. For though death laid hold on Him, yet it did not its own work then.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
